pr.title: feat: Implement Agent Skill System Phase 2

comments.body:
## Unused import Import of 'Any' is not used. --- To fix the problem, remove the unused Any import from the typing module. This eliminates the unnecessary symbol without affecting existing functionality, since the code does not reference Any.

Concretely, in api_service/services/agent_skills_service.py, delete line 7 (from typing import Any). No additional imports, methods, or definitions are required, and no other lines need to change.

## Unused import Import of 'UUID' is not used. --- To fix an unused import, the general solution is to remove that import line (or the unused name from a multi-name import) as long as it is not used anywhere in the file. That preserves functionality while eliminating dead code and satisfying static analysis.

In this file, the single best fix is to delete the from uuid import UUID line at line 8 in api_service/services/agent_skills_service.py. No other code changes are necessary, since there are no references to UUID in the provided snippet. This change is localized to the import block at the top of the file and does not affect existing runtime behavior.

Concretely: in api_service/services/agent_skills_service.py, in the import section at the top, remove the line from uuid import UUID. No new methods, imports, or definitions are needed.

## Unused import Import of 'SkillSetEntry' is not used. --- To fix an unused import, the general approach is to either start using the imported symbol or remove it from the imports. Since SkillSetEntry is not referenced anywhere in this file, the least invasive change is to remove it from the from api_service.db.models import (...) statement while keeping the other imported models intact.

Concretely, in api_service/services/agent_skills_service.py, adjust the multi-line import block starting at line 15 to omit SkillSetEntry. Leave AgentSkillDefinition, AgentSkillFormat, AgentSkillVersion, and SkillSet as they are. No other code changes, new methods, or additional imports are needed.

`Any` and `UUID` are imported but not used in this module. This will trigger Ruff/flake8 unused-import checks; please remove them (or use them if they were intended for type annotations).
```suggestion

```

`format_str` is validated (`AgentSkillFormat(format_str)`) only after the artifact has already been created/written. If `format_str` is invalid (or even `None`), this will raise after side effects and can leave orphaned artifacts. Validate/parse `format_str` before calling the artifact service, and convert invalid values into a clear domain/API error.

If the DB insert fails with `IntegrityError` after writing the artifact (e.g., concurrent duplicate version), the code rolls back but does not clean up the already-created artifact, leaving leaked/orphaned blobs. Consider deleting/marking the artifact failed on rollback (or deferring artifact creation until after the version row is safely persisted).

`format` is typed as `Optional[str]` but defaults to "markdown"; allowing explicit `null` here will likely propagate a `None` into version creation and raise at runtime. If `null` is not meaningful, make this a non-optional `str` (and/or validate against the supported formats).
```suggestion
    format: str = Field("markdown")
```

Alembic `server_default` for Boolean columns is set to the string literals `'true'`/`'false'`. Prefer `sa.true()` / `sa.false()` (or `sa.text('true')` / `sa.text('false')`) to ensure correct DDL across dialects and avoid storing text defaults in SQLite.
```suggestion
    op.add_column('user_profile', sa.Column('agent_skill_repo_sources_enabled', sa.Boolean(), nullable=False, server_default=sa.true()))
    op.add_column('user_profile', sa.Column('agent_skill_local_sources_enabled', sa.Boolean(), nullable=False, server_default=sa.false()))
```

These are `AsyncMock`s, so `assert_called_once()` doesn't guarantee the coroutine was awaited. Prefer `assert_awaited_once()` / `assert_awaited_once_with()` here to ensure the version creation actually awaited the artifact calls.
```suggestion
            # Verify that the artifact service was awaited
            mock_artifact_service.create.assert_awaited_once()
            mock_artifact_service.write_complete.assert_awaited_once()
```

## Pull request overview Implements Phase 2 of the Agent Skill System plan by adding persisted skill definitions/versions and skill sets, plus service-layer CRUD/version publishing helpers and API schema contracts. **Changes:** - Add new DB models + Alembic migration for agent skills, skill versions, skill sets, and user profile feature toggles. - Introduce `AgentSkillsService` to create/list skills, create immutable versions backed by Temporal artifact storage, and create skill sets. - Add Pydantic API schemas and unit tests covering core service behaviors. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `api_service/db/models.py` | Adds ORM models/enums for agent skills, versions, and skill sets; adds user profile flags. | | `api_service/migrations/versions/b47e8ad48d4c_agent_skill_system_phase_2.py` | Creates new tables/indexes/constraints and adds boolean flags to `user_profile`. | | `api_service/services/agent_skills_service.py` | Implements CRUD + version publishing via `TemporalArtifactService`. | | `api_service/api/schemas.py` | Adds request/response schemas for skills, versions, and skill sets. | | `tests/unit/api/test_agent_skills_service.py` | Adds unit tests for create skill/version/skill-set flows and error cases. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The `format` field should be restricted to the supported values defined in the `AgentSkillFormat` enum to ensure API validation catches invalid inputs early.

```suggestion
    format: Optional[Literal["markdown", "bundle"]] = Field("markdown")
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The `AgentSkillSummaryResponse` schema includes a `latest_version` field, but the `AgentSkillDefinition` model lacks a corresponding property. Adding this property will allow Pydantic to correctly populate the field when using `from_attributes=True`.

```python
    @property
    def latest_version(self) -> str | None:
        """Return the version string of the most recently created version."""
        return self.versions[-1].version_string if self.versions else None

```

## Code Review

This pull request implements the core Agent Skill System (Phase 2), introducing database models, Pydantic schemas, and a service layer for managing agent skills, immutable versions, and skill sets. It includes an Alembic migration and comprehensive unit tests. Feedback focuses on improving API validation using Literals, adding missing properties to SQLAlchemy models to support Pydantic's attribute mapping, ensuring consistent session commit/refresh ordering, and simplifying artifact reference logic.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The `SkillSetEntryResponse` schema includes a `skill_slug` field, but the `SkillSetEntry` model lacks a corresponding property. Adding this property will allow Pydantic to correctly populate the field.

```python
    @property
    def skill_slug(self) -> str:
        """Return the slug of the associated skill."""
        return self.skill.slug

```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The order of `refresh` and `commit` is inconsistent with other methods in this service. It is generally safer to `commit()` first and then `refresh()` to ensure the returned object is not expired if the session is configured with `expire_on_commit=True`.

```suggestion
        await self._session.commit()
        await self._session.refresh(skill)
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

This line appears to be redundant or potentially incorrect. If `artifact_ref` is intended to store the unique identifier of the artifact, `artifact.artifact_id` is sufficient. If it is intended to store a full reference string (as suggested by the 1024 character limit in the DB model), then accessing `.artifact_id` on the result of `build_artifact_ref` might be stripping necessary prefix information.

```suggestion
        artifact_ref = artifact.artifact_id
```

## Unused import Import of 'build_artifact_ref' is not used. --- To fix this problem, remove the unused symbol from the import statement while keeping any symbols that are actually used. This eliminates the unnecessary import without affecting runtime behavior.

Concretely, in api_service/services/agent_skills_service.py, the line:

from moonmind.workflows.temporal import (
    TemporalArtifactService,
    build_artifact_ref,
)

should be updated so that only TemporalArtifactService is imported, since that is referenced as the type of artifact_service in __init__. The best minimal change is to remove build_artifact_ref from the import list and keep the multi-line style or collapse to a single line; either is fine functionally, but maintaining a simple, readable single-line import is acceptable and does not change functionality.

No new methods, imports, or definitions are required; this is purely an import cleanup.
